LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. RE: SECTION 1102.5 CAUSE OF ACTION: NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT BECAUSE THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF SUFFERED FROM AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. RE: ASSAULT AND BATTERY CAUSES OF ACTION NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION ACT. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 (301/AJR)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Larry Hecimovich, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3933 for defendant and moving party City and County of San Francisco, Department of Public Works
Kathleen Alvarado, Esq., 415-431-9403 for plaintiff and opposing party James Quinn

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted.
Re: section 1102.5 cause of action: no triable issues of fact because there is insufficient evidence that plaintiff suffered from an adverse employment action.
Re: assault and battery causes of action no triable issues of fact because plaintiff's claim is barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Worker's Compensation Act. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 (301/AJR)
